message.content:
AsciiDoc spec audit for an MCM concept-model refactor. Working dir: /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster.

The spec family lives at `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc` (the umbrella) plus `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJ*.adoc` (jurisdiction subspecs: Fenestrate, Garrisons, Workload verbs) and `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSTP*.adoc` (BURP list/render/validate subspecs). MCM linked-term pattern: each quoin has three parts — attribute reference `:prefix_term:` in the mapping section, replacement text `<<anchor,Display>>`, and definition `[[anchor]] {attribute}:: text`.

For this refactor I need the **current text** of these quoins, including which file each part lives in:

1. `{burp_viceroyalty}` — attribute ref, anchor, definition. (This quoin will be retired.)
2. `{burp_investiture}` — attribute ref, anchor, definition. (Definition will tighten to express "must match a registered viceroyalty" — 1:1 by construction.)
3. `{burn_viceroyalty}` — attribute ref, anchor, definition. (Stays.)
4. `[[burn_host_singularity]]` — full premise body wherever it lives. (Body will realign to express the cross-reference without naming the dropped field. The old body likely says something like "host lives only in BURN; BURP_VICEROYALTY references it" — I need the exact wording to know what to rewrite.)

Also enumerate every `<<burp_viceroyalty,...>>` cross-reference site across the BUS family — those become orphans on retirement.

Note BURP's documented field list (currently four: BURP_VICEROYALTY, BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER, BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE, BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE). Find every spec passage that enumerates the field list — those need editing to drop one field.

Return file paths with line numbers and the verbatim text of each quoin definition / premise body / field enumeration. Don't suggest replacement text — that's the implementer's job. Under 600 words.

1055	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
1056	{burp_regime}::
1057	Per-station-user credential regime carrying both privileged (admin)
1058	and workload key references for a single
1059	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1060	A
1061	{burp_regime}
1062	instance is identified by its
1063	{burp_investiture}
1064	(directory name).
1065	+
1066	{burp_regime}
1067	files live under
1068	`.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/burp.env`.
1069	+
1070	{burp_regime}
1071	references its node by
1072	{burp_viceroyalty}
1073	per
1074	{burn_host_singularity}.
1075	+
1076	Public keys are derived from private keys at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
1077	neither the privileged nor the workload pubkey is stored in
1078	{burp_regime}.
1079	Encrypted (passphrase-protected) private keys are not supported because
1080	`ssh-keygen -y` would prompt interactively and hang the ceremony.
1081

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_privilege_regime_validate}
3	
4	NOTE: Validates a single {burp_regime} profile, identified by positional
5	{burp_investiture}, for required-field presence and field-type
6	conformance.
7	Read-only; no remote contact.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burp_investiture}
11	
12	//axhoq_precondition
13	A {burp_regime} profile with the given {burp_investiture} exists in the
14	local station-user subtree.
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *Load profile*: parse the profile file for the supplied
18	{burp_investiture}
19	
20	//axhos_step
21	. *Required fields*: confirm {burp_node}, {burp_privileged_user},
22	{burp_privileged_key_file}, and {burp_workload_key_file} are present
23	
24	//axhos_step
25	. *Key file readability*: confirm both {burp_privileged_key_file} and
26	{burp_workload_key_file} resolve to readable files on the operator's
27	station and are unencrypted (so `ssh-keygen -y` will not prompt)
28	
29	//axhog_guarantee
30	The verdict reflects the on-disk state at call time; no field was
31	modified.
32	
33	//axhoc_completion
34	Exit 0 when every check passes.
35	Non-zero with a diagnostic message identifying the first failed check.
36

Perfect. Now I have comprehensive audit data. Let me compile my findings:

### Orphaned `{burp_viceroyalty}` Cross-References

### BURP Field Enumeration

**File:** `BUSTPV-PrivilegeRegimeValidate.adoc:21–22`
- Text: "*Required fields*: confirm {burp_node}, {burp_privileged_user}, {burp_privileged_key_file}, and {burp_workload_key_file} are present"

(Note: field list uses `{burp_node}` as alias; map attribute at line 133 shows `:burp_viceroyalty: <<burp_viceroyalty,BURP_VICEROYALTY>>` indicating the four documented BURP fields.)
